# Unit Testing Instructions

- Write tests for observable behavior and critical edge cases.
- Keep tests small, readable, and independent of each other.
- Prefer deterministic setups and avoid unnecessary mocks.
- Name tests clearly to describe the scenario and expected outcome.
- Cover success paths, failure paths, and boundary conditions.
- Avoid asserting on implementation details when behavior can be asserted directly.
- Refactor test setup into helpers when duplication becomes repetitive.
- Fix the root cause of failures rather than weakening assertions.
